title: Eva Epiphanies or Was it obviousor am I just slow
display_title: Eva Epiphanies or Was it obviousor am I just slow
wikitext:
[]: Eva Epiphanies (or, Was it obvious...or am I just slow?)             Quote Post by Damaijin » Mon Nov 11, 2013 7:18 pm I was just watching Evangelion 2.0. When it got to the part where Mari pulls Shinji out of the shelter and they watch Rei and 00 get absorbed into Zeruel, Shinji looked down at the blood on his hand and then took off to pilot 01. I've watched the whole movie more times than is probably mentally healthy, but for some reason when I saw it this time, it totally clicked that it was a direct callback to the part in the first film where Shinji saw Rei's blood on his hand and it motivated him to get in the Eva! So, did everyone else pick up on that, or am I just slow? And what about you? Have you ever had an Eva epiphany that you want to share?

[]:             Quote Post by monitoradiation » Mon Nov 11, 2013 8:36 pm Yes I think that scene is supposed to mirror the 1.11 "I mustn't run away" scene. Adding to that, he's literally looking at Eva-02's blood (Asuka's unit) so there's also a metaphorical "blood on his hands" guilt he's feeling that he has to atone for.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Mon Nov 11, 2013 8:38 pm Hand symbolism is very apparent throughout the series. I haven't been able to find a wiki thread about it, oddly enough.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Nov 11, 2013 9:12 pm I'm not sure if "epiphany" is the word I'd use, but I've been noticing things about Eva , including things that are purely the product of my own deranged pareidolia, for about 13 years. Any kind of list would be unfeasible. It might not be an exaggeration to say that the current face of English language Eva fanwankery would look very different had I never showed up to share my weird observations and ideas, but who really knows or cares.

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Mon Nov 11, 2013 9:45 pm Stryker wrote: Hand symbolism is very apparent throughout the series. I haven't been able to find a wiki thread about it, oddly enough.
